title: Quiz 7
subtitle: B1-B2 Bridge Challenge
source: Original B1-B2 quiz created for this system

questions.question: When the coach arrived, the team _____ for nearly an hour.
questions.explanation: Past perfect continuous uses had been + verb-ing. It shows an action in progress before another past event.

questions.question: By this time tomorrow, we _____ our science presentation.
questions.explanation: Future perfect uses will have + past participle. It looks back from a future time to a completed action.

questions.question: If I had checked the timetable, I _____ the wrong bus.
questions.explanation: Third conditional: if + past perfect, would not have + past participle. It talks about an unreal past result.

questions.question: If I had gone to bed earlier, I _____ so tired now.
questions.explanation: This is a mixed conditional: a past action affects the present. Use if + past perfect, would + base verb now.

questions.question: I wish my brother _____ borrowing my headphones without asking.
questions.explanation: Use wish + would for annoying habits or things we want someone to change.

questions.question: It _____ that exercise helps people concentrate better.
questions.explanation: Formal reporting structures often use passive forms: It is believed that..., It is said that...

questions.question: My uncle, _____ lives in Canada, is visiting us next month.
questions.explanation: In non-defining relative clauses about people, use who. The commas show extra information.

questions.question: Take a raincoat _____ it gets cold later.
questions.explanation: In case means 'because something might happen'. You prepare before the possible problem.

questions.question: You can borrow my camera _____ you promise to be careful with it.
questions.explanation: Provided that means 'only if'. It gives a condition.

questions.question: My sister loves noisy places, _____ I prefer quiet cafes.
questions.explanation: Whereas compares two different facts or preferences.

questions.question: The lights are off and nobody is answering. They _____ gone out.
questions.explanation: Must have + past participle is used for a strong guess about the past.

questions.question: Mum _____ her car washed yesterday.
questions.explanation: Have something done means someone else does the action for you: had her car washed.

questions.question: She asked me _____ I had finished my homework.
questions.explanation: For yes/no questions in reported speech, use if or whether.

questions.question: I regret _____ so much money on that game.
questions.explanation: Regret + -ing means you feel sorry about something you did in the past.

questions.question: We had to _____ the picnic because the weather was terrible.
questions.explanation: Put off means postpone or do something later than planned.

questions.question: Our group needs to _____ a better idea for the project.
questions.explanation: Come up with means think of or produce an idea.

questions.question: Even though the homework was hard, Nina decided to _____ until she finished.
questions.explanation: Carry on means continue.

questions.question: This website is very _____. I use it when I need correct information. (rely)
questions.explanation: Rely is a verb. Reliable is an adjective meaning something or someone you can trust.

questions.question: Children often ask questions because of their natural _____. (curious)
questions.explanation: Curious is an adjective. Curiosity is the noun.

questions.question: We _____ for twenty minutes before the bus finally arrived. (wait)
questions.explanation: Use had been + verb-ing for an action continuing up to a past moment.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: By the time we arrived the train had already left
questions.explanation: Use past perfect for the earlier past action: the train had already left.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: I wish I had remembered to bring my camera
questions.explanation: I wish + past perfect is used for regrets about the past.

questions.reading: Amelia's class entered a robotics fair with a small machine that could sort plastic bottle caps by colour. At first, the machine kept dropping the caps, and some classmates wanted to start again with a simpler idea. Amelia suggested changing the angle of the ramp instead. After several tests, the machine worked smoothly. The judges did not give her team first prize, but they praised the group for solving problems creatively.
questions.question: What problem did the machine have at first?
questions.explanation: The text says the machine kept dropping the caps.
options: It sorted paper instead of plastic

questions.reading: Amelia's class entered a robotics fair with a small machine that could sort plastic bottle caps by colour. At first, the machine kept dropping the caps, and some classmates wanted to start again with a simpler idea. Amelia suggested changing the angle of the ramp instead. After several tests, the machine worked smoothly. The judges did not give her team first prize, but they praised the group for solving problems creatively.
questions.question: What does Amelia's suggestion show about her?
questions.explanation: She looked for a practical solution instead of giving up, so she was a good problem-solver.
options: She is good at solving problems

questions.reading: Amelia's class entered a robotics fair with a small machine that could sort plastic bottle caps by colour. At first, the machine kept dropping the caps, and some classmates wanted to start again with a simpler idea. Amelia suggested changing the angle of the ramp instead. After several tests, the machine worked smoothly. The judges did not give her team first prize, but they praised the group for solving problems creatively.
questions.question: How did the judges probably feel about the team?
questions.explanation: They did not win, but the judges praised their creative problem-solving.

questions.question: I would love to buy that bike, but I can't _____ it.
questions.explanation: Afford means have enough money to buy or do something.

questions.question: Hardly _____ down when the phone rang.
questions.explanation: After hardly at the beginning of a sentence, use inversion: Hardly had I sat down when...

questions.question: When we were younger, we _____ spend hours building forts in the garden.
questions.explanation: Would can describe repeated past actions and habits, especially with a time phrase like when we were younger.

questions.question: I wrote the instructions clearly _____ everyone could follow them.
questions.explanation: So that introduces purpose: the reason for doing something.

questions.question: If you had called me, I _____ you. (help)
questions.explanation: Third conditional result: would have + past participle.

questions.question: _____ the fact that he was tired, Leo kept practising.
questions.explanation: Use in spite of the fact that + clause. Despite can also be used in the same way.

questions.question: You may use the computer _____ you save your work in the right folder.
questions.explanation: Provided that means only if. It introduces a condition.

questions.question: He speaks English as if he _____ in London for years.
questions.explanation: As if can use past perfect for an imaginary past experience: as if he had lived there.

questions.question: The app is not only useful _____ also fun to use.
questions.explanation: The fixed pair is not only... but also.

questions.question: _____ by the noise, the baby started crying.
questions.explanation: A past participle clause can show cause: Frightened by the noise = Because the baby was frightened.

questions.question: Everyone has a _____ to protect the environment. (responsible)
questions.explanation: Responsible is an adjective. Responsibility is the noun.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: If I had known about the test I would have studied earlier
questions.explanation: Third conditional: If + past perfect, would have + past participle.

questions.reading: During a class debate, students discussed whether homework should be done online or on paper. Kai said online homework was better because teachers could give feedback quickly. Aisha disagreed. She said that when everything was online, she got distracted by messages and games. Their teacher did not choose a winner. Instead, she asked the class to design a homework plan that used technology only when it genuinely helped learning.
questions.question: Why did Kai prefer online homework?
questions.explanation: Kai said teachers could give feedback quickly.

questions.reading: During a class debate, students discussed whether homework should be done online or on paper. Kai said online homework was better because teachers could give feedback quickly. Aisha disagreed. She said that when everything was online, she got distracted by messages and games. Their teacher did not choose a winner. Instead, she asked the class to design a homework plan that used technology only when it genuinely helped learning.
questions.question: Why did Aisha dislike doing everything online?
questions.explanation: She said she got distracted by messages and games.

questions.reading: During a class debate, students discussed whether homework should be done online or on paper. Kai said online homework was better because teachers could give feedback quickly. Aisha disagreed. She said that when everything was online, she got distracted by messages and games. Their teacher did not choose a winner. Instead, she asked the class to design a homework plan that used technology only when it genuinely helped learning.
questions.question: What was the teacher's main idea?
questions.explanation: The teacher wanted a balanced plan: use technology when it truly helps learning.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: Neither of the answers is singular in careful English, so use is.
options: Neither of the answers are correct.
Neither of the answers is correct.

questions.question: There is _____ evidence that sleep helps memory.
questions.explanation: Evidence is uncountable, so use a great deal of or much, not many.

questions.question: _____ you practise, _____ confident you become.
questions.explanation: The + comparative..., the + comparative... shows two things changing together.

questions.question: _____, our outdoor concert was cancelled because of the storm. (fortunate)
questions.explanation: Fortunate means lucky. Unfortunately is the adverb meaning unluckily.

questions.question: A good team member should _____ responsibility for their work.
questions.explanation: The natural collocation is take responsibility.

questions.question: We are _____ to hand in the project by Friday.
questions.explanation: Be supposed to means be expected to or should do something.

questions.question: The new plan is a big _____ on the old one. (improve)
questions.explanation: Improve is a verb. Improvement is a noun.

questions.question: Your answer is almost right, but it is not completely _____.
questions.explanation: Accurate means correct and exact.

questions.question: Put the words in the correct order:
questions.correctSentence: The more I practise the more confident I become
questions.explanation: This pattern shows two linked changes: the more..., the more...

questions.question: Which sentence sounds most natural?
questions.explanation: Look forward to is followed by the -ing form: looking forward to meeting.
options: I look forward to meet you.
I look forward to meeting you.
